behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a local module boundary (request builder -> API client payload), so a wired in-process test is needed. Rejected Unit because the behavior text is about the request actually sent, not just a pure helper. Rejected System/Agentic because no real OS/network/model judgment is required; the check is payload completeness.
behaviors.test_description: Run the classifier with a stubbed API transport and assert the outbound request payload contains every configured taxonomy label (no omissions, no partial list) for multiple taxonomy sizes.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears when provider output is handed off into module normalization/validation and returned to caller. Rejected Unit because behavior text covers end-to-end module response after API output. Rejected Agentic because this check does not depend on model quality, only deterministic guard logic for out-of-taxonomy labels.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API response with invalid labels (case variants, whitespace, unknown token) and assert classifier returns exactly `unknown` to callers instead of raw provider value.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text isolates one local transformation: normalization determinism on identical input. No module handoff, I/O, or model call needed.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization repeatedly with identical raw response fixtures and assert identical normalized output each run (including casing/spacing/punctuation variants in fixtures).

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly depends on repeated live model calls and possible non-deterministic provider behavior. A mock would hide real drift/inconsistency. Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes repeated executions of one agentic step, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Against the real provider, issue repeated classifications for fixed (document, taxonomy), record raw provider outputs and final labels, and fail if final label changes while raw provider decision is unchanged.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Need wiring between token-budget logic and dispatch boundary (enforce-before-send). Rejected Unit because behavior text includes pre-dispatch enforcement effect, not only token counting math. Rejected System because in-process stubs can verify truncation/rejection and whether dispatch was blocked.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small budget, run classifier with oversized document, and assert policy outcome (`truncate` sends reduced payload or `reject` prevents send) using a stub transport that captures whether dispatch occurred and with what payload.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Requires real provider behavior (actual model-side tokenization/usage accounting). Mocked usage can always be made compliant and would hide real over-budget failures. Rejected Workflow because this is a single call behavior, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run classification on representative real documents against the live provider, collect provider-reported token usage metadata, and assert each call is <= configured budget; fail and surface prompt/doc combinations that exceed budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is retry orchestration across local modules (error handling, wait, retry, success propagation). Rejected System because real network/rate limits are not required to reproduce this logic; controlled stub responses and clock are sufficient. Rejected Agentic because correctness is independent of model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub API to return rate-limit error then success; assert module waits per backoff policy, retries expected number of times, and returns successful label instead of immediate error.
